items.title: Fastest Way to Farm Sirius and Orion Blueprints in Warframe
items.summary: From the Constellations quest to the Pontis Tower vendor, here is my direct strategic guide to unlocking Warframe
items.content:
Digital Extremes has finally decided that managing one space ninja is too easy, so now you have to babysit two of them simultaneously.

I've seen plenty of weird experimental designs roll out over the years, but Sirius and Orion are completely detached from the usual blueprint structure. They're two brothers sharing the same thread of fate, meaning you literally can't select one without dragging the other into battle. They play simultaneously, which means you have to build both of them to use the character at all. If you want to skip the confusion and add this dual threat to your arsenal, I've mapped out the exact farming loops and token economies required to bypass the worst of the friction.

Before you fire up your engines and blindly fly into deep space, you have to clear some narrative hurdles.

You can't jump straight into the starchart to look for these parts without finishing the foundational story steps first. The entire farm is locked behind a specific set of nodes, and you need to clear the path before the game even permits you to look at the drop tables.

Your first real step is finishing the Jade Shadows: Constellations quest. It's a direct sequel to the original Jade Shadows storyline, so you need to wrap that up before you can even think about the brothers. The good news is that Constellations is incredibly brief, taking roughly half an hour from start to finish. There's absolutely no random number generation or hidden grind here. You finish the quest and the game hands you the main Sirius and Orion blueprint completely free of charge.

Clearing that quest also unlocks Pontis Tower, a brand new orbital hub located around Uranus. This structure serves as your absolute base of operations for everything related to the new frames. Don't ignore this location, because it houses the specific merchants and pity systems you'll need later when your luck inevitably fails.

Once your quest log is clear, the real farming loop takes you directly into the trenches of Uranus Proxima.

You'll need to hunt down the actual component blueprints in open space. Every craftable frame requires three specific components alongside your main document: the Chassis, the Neuroptics, and the Systems. Every single one of these blueprints has a flat 14.29% drop chance from the new nodes, meaning you can expect a decent amount of repetition before your foundry is fully populated.

You'll be fighting Vena and Ryoku, the two protoframes you bumped into during the main storyline. They hold the keys to your loot drops and appear across two distinct mission choices on your map: Scoria's Angel and the Kuva Wytch.

Before you panic about your fragile hull integrity taking a beating in high level content, you can make use of a massive built-in safety net. If you've completely ignored your personal ship upgrades and fly a literal tin can, you can actually hijack Scoria's Angel or the Kuva Wytch themselves during the skirmish. These capital ships are fully kitted out with high tier modifications. They come pre-manned by Vena's minions or Ryoku's Shadow Clones, letting you finish the fight with stolen firepower.

Before you waste hours worrying about which node to choose, know that both missions draw from the exact same component pool. You can get any of the three blueprints from either boss. The only real reason to pick one over the other is if you're actively target farming their signature weapons, the Pride and Wrath Heavy Scythe, or looking for specific token types.

If the drop tables refuse to hand over that last blueprint, you can buy your way out of the space casino using tokens.

Completing the Uranus Proxima skirmishes rewards you with Emerald Tokens and Crimson Tokens, occasionally called Talents, depending on which boss you decide to bully. You can take these currencies to the hidden vendor tucked inside Pontis Tower to buy the exact blueprints you need.

To help you plan your resource allocations, I put together the exact pricing structure for the token market below:

items.title: Warframe Creators Unveil New Missions and First Dual Warframe
items.summary: The developers will continue the old story and give players a reason to return to Railjack.
items.content:
0 Источник: Digital Extremes 20 Jun 22:59 The developers will continue the old story and give players a reason to return to Railjack.

Digital Extremes studio announced the release of the “Jade Shadows: Constellations” update for Warframe. A lot of new content has been added to the game.

Players can expect a cinematic narrative quest that “will pick up where Jade Shadows left off in 2024”:

Once again, take control of the Stalker as he once again allies with the Tenno amidst the conflict in the celestial dimensions. Sons from a parallel future, warring with each other, have already arrived in the Origin System in search of their father, and they will stop at nothing to include him in their timelines. The first dual Warframe, “Sirius and Orion,” was also introduced (they will share one slot in the player's arsenal). The characters use the scythes Pride and Wrath and possess different abilities. For example, Orion can create a drifting black hole, while Sirius can create a light source that will heal and revive allies.

In addition, new Railjack missions have appeared. After completing the quest, players will be able to unlock “Uranus Proxima,” where they can complete “the most accessible Railjack missions to date” — they do not require ranking up or owning their own ship.

The full description of the “Jade Shadows: Constellations” update for Warframe is available at the following link.

items.title: What is the Mesa Heirloom release date in Warframe?
items.content:
Guide What is the Mesa Heirloom release date in Warframe? Hold your horses, cowboy.

The Heirloom skins in Warframe are designed to bring new life to older designs. It very much has its own style, with swirling energy on display inside the body of the Warframe, making it wholly unique when compared to every other skin in the game.

The Mesa Heirloom skin will launch on July 11 and will be available for real-world currency or for Platinum. They can be bought as individual skins or components for Platinum, or as a bundle including additional Platinum if you would prefer to spend real money.

Be sure to check out the rest of our Warframe news and guides.

Shacknews staff does not use generative artificial intelligence (AI) in their content. Shacknews strictly prohibits the use of its content for AI training or to generate text, including text in the style or format used for this publication. Shacknews reserves all rights to this work.

Hailing from Ireland, Aidan has been conditioned by local weather conditions to survive hours at his PC grinding through whatever game is offering the lowest possible drop rates for loot. He thinks the easiest way to figure out what fans of games want to read is to just be a fan of games. You can normally find him logged into Warframe, Destiny, or a gacha game. You can reach out to him on X @scannerbarkly .

Aidan O'Brien posted a new article, What is the Mesa Heirloom release date in Warframe?

items.title: Warframe of Mind: Shining some light on Jade Shadows Constellations
items.content:
Yes, I feel like the Warframe developers are being a little overly responsive to memes at the moment. I know that there have been a whole lot of jokes about how the game is for the moms, so now we have an update that’s just All The Dads. Out of nowhere, we get an additional dad on top of existing dad drama, and I half expected for Father to show up even though there’s nothing going on remotely related to Deimos just to have all the dads in one place .

I am being a little snarky, of course, but I do appreciate that this particular update comes with a narrative quest and not just some new gameplay elements, which isn’t always a given. And frankly, I feel like this one had some stakes riding on it compared to other updates to the game. After all, Follie’s update was not precisely received warmly aside from the frame herself. So how are we doing now that we’re following up on a pretty unique storyline?

Warning: While I am not going to spoil the events of the quest directly, I will be referencing the major characters involved and the resolution in oblique terms. This may involve some spoilers along the way. If you haven’t done the quest yet and wish to avoid any spoilers, skip ahead to the first image break.

First and foremost, I appreciate the stuff that this particular update doesn’t bother with. It does not explain how Sirius and Orion grew up or the mechanics of that, nor does it content seriously with the idea of a fully natural Warframe infant in any sense. (This might be explored when the frame gets a Prime; it might not.) It doesn’t even really engage with perhaps the biggest deliberate question mark of how the boys traveled into the past in the first place.

Why are they here? Because they are. We already know that time travel is possible and, in fact, have known this for quite some time. Why does anything happen? Just move past it.

Speaking personally, I had picked Sirius back when I first did the quest, and I suspect that alters some dialogue, but we are spared any period of time in which we treat this like it is an ambiguous or vague scenario. The Stalker needs to protect his son, and just as the current baby is his son, both of these adults are his son. There isn’t a matter of one or the other. He needs to resolve this scenario.

And what a resolution we get. As mentioned above, I am trying to avoid serious spoilers, but not only is one major character implied to be out of commission, it’s at least somewhat possible that two are. (The third… doesn’t seem to be? I feel like the story kind of forgets about our Guest Star Dad a little bit, although given the randomness of his inclusion, this is forgivable.) I hope we get more material following up on the consequences of this change, even just as incidental dialogue in the future.

Now, as in most narrative quests, you wind up being handed a specific Warframe to play the whole time… and unfortunately, it’s the Stalker. I loathe playing as the Stalker. I don’t enjoy Dread or Despair as weapons, which is why I don’t use them. I don’t like playing as Ash (with whom he shares 50% of his abilities). He doesn’t even have some useful new synergies with any of these abilities! He’s just a variant on Ash with fewer words!

The part that makes this somewhat less onerous is, of course, that it makes perfect sense for us to be controlling him. But it also becomes somewhat more onerous insofar as we have less reason than ever to not be helping the Stalker directly. I realize I’m grousing over a comparatively minor thing here, but it does stand out to me, just like the fact that the new game modes offer us two variants on a scythe for the weapon reward.

I mean, given this family, a bow and throwing knives would just make sense, you feel me? Ah well.

Once you’ve finished the quest – which is pretty straightforward but fun, which is about what I hope for from this content – you get access to the new Railjack missions, and I have to admit this makes me grouse a little bit just because… I do actually have a developed Railjack, and I want to be able to use it instead of the Railjack belonging to the NPCs. It’s a fairly minor thing, but it does have an impact, since queueing up with a group means you also don’t get progress toward affinity on your own Railjack. (You do gain points for Intrinsics, though.) The actual missions themselves are nicely extended, though, and I understand why they’re structured like this.

Also, the boss fights are… fairly all right, by Warframe standards! They don’t feel like unfair nonsense but also don’t feel pointlessly easy or meandering. And it would have been easy to make Ryoku and Vena mirrors of one another, but no, each of them have very distinct boss fights and equivalent but not identical missions to fight them.

I am left with a lot of questions about Ryoku and Vena, seeing as how in a strict sense neither of them is “technically” a protoframe (they’re from the future, not the past, and from a time when both Garuda and Ash already exist). There are also loads of questions about them individually, which neither is inclined to answer in any particular detail. Vena has some connection to the Grineer and Kuva, obviously, and at one point she refers to herself being in a romantic relationship with Orion; by contrast, Ryoku is a bit more taciturn, but there’s still some obvious backstory there we don’t know about. And some of it we don’t need to know, but I’m curious!

For that matter, there’s the obvious question about what, exactly, led to their doomed futures. It’s obvious that things unfolded differently in both timeframes, but in both timelines when Sirius or Orion grow up, there appears to be a doom point. What is that point? Does it imply that the Stalker won in these timeline and killed the player? Or is there something else going on?

items.title: Jade Shadows: Constellations Official Soundtrack
items.summary: Fitting for a tectonic brawl between cosmic titans, the Jade Shadows: Constellations Soundtrack features radical rock riffs and catalyzing choral cacophonies. Sirius & Orion may be locked in conflict, but you can bask in harmony with Warfra
items.content:
Fitting for a tectonic brawl between cosmic titans, the Jade Shadows: Constellations Soundtrack features radical rock riffs and catalyzing choral cacophonies. Sirius & Orion may be locked in conflict, but you can bask in harmony with Warframe's latest musical tracks, exquisitely performed and produced in-house by our talented Sound Team.

Where can I listen?

You can find the Jade Shadows: Constellations Official Soundtrack on:
  • Spotify
  • Apple Music
  • YouTube Music
  • Amazon Music
  • Tidal
  • Deezer
  • Bandcamp
Additionally, we've created a new playlist with help from UNFD, the record label who helped us bring ERRA's "Crawl Backwards Out of Heaven" to our unforgettable Jade Shadows: Constellations animated short. Find Warframe x UNFD on Spotify and listen to a specially-curated assortment of tracks from Warframe, ERRA and other artists.

FULL TRACKLIST:

1. Celestial Clash
  • Composer: Matthew Chalmers
  • Producers: Erich Preston, Matthew Chalmers
  • Choir:Anaïs Kelsey-Verdecchia, Rayna Crandlemire, Kayla Ruiz, Anika Venkatesh, Sarah Mole, Francesca Hauser, Joshua Sutherland, Nathan Gritter, Tyrese Walters, Kai Leung, Martin Gomes, Henry Paterson
  • Choir Orchestration: Francesca Hauser
  • Choir Engineer: Stephen Koszler
2. Sacred Light
  • Composer: Matthew Chalmers
  • Producers: Erich Preston, Matthew Chalmers
  • Choir: Anaïs Kelsey-Verdecchia, Rayna Crandlemire, Kayla Ruiz, Anika Venkatesh, Sarah Mole, Francesca Hauser, Joshua Sutherland, Nathan Gritter, Tyrese Walters, Kai Leung, Martin Gomes, Henry Paterson
  • Choir Orchestration: Francesca Hauser
  • Choir Engineer: Stephen Koszler
  • Koto: Ray Hickey
  • Shakuhachi: Ron Korb
  • Lyrics: Matthew Chalmers
LYRICS: Where sacred light shines The song of life begins As the shadow of time The protector marches evermore towards silence Glory on high Where the sacred light shines

items.title: Our Refreshed Refer a Friend Program
items.summary: Make note, Tenno — Warframe’s Refer a Friend program update is now live! You can take a look at all the need-to-know details below. Referral Code Changes You can now create and send referral codes from any platform with a cross platform sav
items.content:
Make note, Tenno — Warframe’s Refer a Friend program update is now live! You can take a look at all the need-to-know details below.

Referral Code Changes

You can now create and send referral codes from any platform with a cross platform save enabled account! All you need to do is log into your account on Warframe.com, generate a referral code from the Refer a Friend page, then send that code to the friend you are trying to recruit. After your friend creates their Warframe account, they have seven days to qualify as your recruit. Your friend then inputs your referral code and links your accounts together by either:
  • Entering the referral code in-game by hitting "Communication > Friends > Refer Tenno".
  • Entering the referral code on the Account Management page.

Reward Changes

Both recruiters and recruits can earn Rewards through the new Refer a Friend system.

Recruiter Rewards

There are now four Reward tiers for recruiters to earn depending on how many referrals they send! Recruit 1 Tenno
  • 3x Forma
  • Orokin Catalyst
  • Orokin Reactor
  • Exilus Weapon Adapter
  • Exilus Warframe Adapter
  • Dream Catcher Honoria
Recruit 2 Tenno
  • Two Weapon Slots
Recruit 3 Tenno
  • Warframe Slot
Recruit 4 Tenno
  • Two Archwing Weapon Slots
  • Gravimag
Please note: Once your recruit completes "The Teacher" Quest, you will unlock your recruiter reward! The more Tenno you recruit, the more rewards you get! These Rewards are also retroactive for anyone that qualifies.

Recruit Rewards

After recruits successfully link their accounts, they will instantly gain the following in-game rewards:
  • 7-Day Affinity Booster
  • Classic Color Palette
  • Clan Key

Additional Rewards

Every time one of your recruits makes their first Platinum purchase, 10 percent of the value of Platinum they purchased will be automatically added to your account.

items.title: "Our players can swing different types of ways" - Warframe studio talks avoiding Protoframe burnout, and fending off fans' furious appetite for romance
items.summary: An interview with Digital Extremes community director and live ops lead Megan Everett protoframes.
items.content:
Image credit: Digital Extremes Feature by Connor Makar Staff Writer Published on June 18, 2026 7 comments Follow Warframe Warframe 's next update - Jade Shadows: Constellations - has finally arrived, bringing with it two new Protoframes: Ryoku and Vena. A relatively recent addition to the sci-fi shooter's playable repertoire, Protoframes - essentially human versions of the game's familiar Warframes - were a bold swing that won hearts and minds a few years back, and they've remained popular ever since.

When Protoframes were introduced in 2024's Warframe: 1999 expansion, they were accompanied by an in-game romance system to much acclaim. Once players got a taste, they've desired more ever since - but Jade Shadows: Constellations' new Protoframes are different.

"What's interesting about Ryoku and Vena is I'm sure people will feel a certain way about them not being romanceable, but they can't all be romanceable," community director and live ops lead Megan Everett told Eurogamer.

This time around, Warframe's new Protoframes play a mentor role for the stars of the update: Warframe brothers Orion and Sirius. Rather than attempting to woo Ryoku and Vena, players can side with one over the other, flying off for a space battle that concludes with a tense boss fight against your chosen foe. This is quite the drastic deviation from the norm with Protoframes, something Everett believes is necessary, even if she understands the demand for love.

"You have to branch out with Protoframes to make sure they still have that freshness, and dabble with what players like. But, I already see feedback about people wanting romance - I think everyone will always want romanceable Protoframes.

"I know if my favourite Warframe got a Protoframe, and I wasn't able to do anything like that, I'd be so upset. But if you play the Constellations quest, you'll understand why these characters are so linked to Sirius and Orion, and why they're not an option to be romanced."

It is worth noting that while a desire for romance is ever-present with Protoframes, it hasn't always been included with the introduction of new ones. During last year's Techrot Encore update, for instance, the game introduced four characters, each with their own narratives that meant romance didn't always make sense. Two of them were divorced, for example.

For Everett, while the presence of romanceable Protoframes has been good for the game, it cannot be so present that it begins to lose its lustre. "Our players love the romance systems - it shows our players can swing different types of ways," she explains. "They aren't all around the metagame; not just about the grind. They are open to different gameplay avenues. That's beneficial to us - the lore ones, romanceable ones, and now these mentors. We don't want to have this Protoframe burnout - if we did it for every one we released people would get annoyed."

There's also the challenge of throwing in an ever-growing collection of romanceable characters to an audience who may already feel happy with a pre-established in-game relationship:

"You can't make players care about a character so much, and then throw them another character they can date," Everett says. "When we released the Triad, we had to emphasise you could still date the Warframe 1999 characters, to make sure people wouldn't feel icky and gross. People got really attached to Protoframes, we're beyond appreciative. We just want to avoid frustration wherever possible."

This concern stretches beyond just the topic of romance, however. Too much of a good thing can have its downsides, and "Protoframe burnout" is an ever-present concern on the minds of Warframe's developers. In Everett's words, they have to "remain special".

"We do four new Warframes a year, plus Prime Warframes. Adding Protoframes into the mix in a consistent way would lead to burnout. So we have to pick-and-choose, and have a strategy for where they go. They have to make sense thematically, and fit into the narrative. It's not to say that Protoframes in the future won't be datable, or some won't have a divorce arc or whatever, but things must stay fresh."

items.title: Warframe's latest update is all about space combat, constellations, and cosmic mythology
items.summary: With its latest major update, Digital Extremes makes space for old mechanics and new ideas in the cosmos.
items.content:
Space Games Warframe's latest update is all about space combat, constellations, and cosmic mythology News By Luke Kemp last updated 18 June 2026 With its latest major update, Digital Extremes makes space for old mechanics and new ideas in the cosmos.

When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. Here’s how it works .

Click for next article (Image credit: Digital Extremes) Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Defying recent live service trends in gaming, Warframe has been going strong for 13 years. It's one of the best space games out there, and it shows no signs of slowing down with the Jade Shadows: Constellations update.

The online sci-fi action RPG has evolved both technically and narratively over the years, changing from a game where the story has blossomed from background to the emotion-driven forefront of every major update. In 2024, Jade Shadows started the process of humanising Stalker - the player's long-term nemesis - by making him a father.

Jade Shadows: Constellations serves the dual purpose of continuing this story, and taking the first steps down the road of reinvigorating the game's space-based content.

Watch full video here: At the end of Jade Shadows, players chose one of two names for Stalker's son; Sirius, or Orion. The surprise hook for Constellations is that both came to be… in a way. Bursting onto the scene from parallel timelines, each has come to claim their father for their own. Sirius takes after their mother, while Orion takes after their father. Both are named after notable stellar phenomena: Sirius is the brightest star in the Earth's night sky, while Orion is one of the most prominent constellations visible from Earth (itself named after the legendary hunter in Greek mythology).

Stalker, when he appears during a mission, will relentlessly chase down the player until he or his target dies. What more appropriate name for the son that takes after him, therefore, than the constellation of the Hunter?

Fantasy game 'Crimson Desert' lets you stab your way up to space, showcasing a remarkably detailed cosmos 'SOL Shogunate' is a samurai space opera set on the moon, but with an aerospace engineer making sure it's 'getting the science right' Upcoming space & sci-fi games for 2026 & beyond: Saros, Warhammer 40K: Dawn of War 4, Halo: Campaign Evolved & more

"We thought one of the most impactful ways to end a quest would be letting the player name the child," explains Creative Director Rebecca Ford, "and we held that close to our hearts [...] And these names must come from the cosmos, they must come from space. Then we knew we were going to start going divergent paths pretty quickly."

Warframe's missions are spread across the Origin System, which looks very similar to our own solar system . Players jump between planets and moons with real-life equivalents such as Mars , Deimos , Neptune , Mercury , and Earth. The star Sirius and the constellation Orion also exist in the Warframe universe, and factor into the new quest; although exactly how is under wraps at time of writing.

Signup to our newsletter Follow us on Google Constellations is also being used as a springboard for refreshing the game's space battle Railjack content. "I absolutely felt that this update would not work without a Railjack component for the post quest content," explains Ford.

"It did not feel to me like you could do all of this grand space cosmos-like effort without giving players somewhere to use their Railjack. That is a very, very difficult choice in this line of work, where you need to be able to do things very well and very quickly.

"Railjack [is] such a complicated feature, like the code, and the streaming… everything about it is hard, it's making Warframe on hard mode," reveals Ford.

These are the 7 best sci-fi games I saw at Summer Game Fest 2026 Lunar Strike offers a pessimistic, and worryingly realistic look at the future of space travel 'The Expanse: Osiris Reborn' has lots of 'Mass Effect' DNA, but its developers are teasing a far more grounded sci-fi RPG "In this case we said all right, let's just commit, because we have help from our partners at Sumo, who are co-developing this update, and they love Railjack. So that helped! We just had the stubbornness to say it has to feel this big, it needs this; because it needs to acknowledge that that is how big of an event this quest is."

items.title: Warframe’s New Narrative Chapter Jade Shadows: Constellations Celebrates Father’s Day Early On All Platforms Today
items.summary: 'Jade Shadows: Constellations' narrative chapter the perfect free gift for Father's Day.
items.content:
LONDON, Ontario – Jun. 17, 2026 – Digital Extremes ’ latest Narrative Chapter for Warframe , Jade Shadows: Constellations , is here to pay homage to hard-working dads everywhere for free on all platforms today. Step back into the role of a newfound father, the Stalker, and bring an end to the fight between two versions of his son in an emotional Quest, inspired by adrenaline-pumping shonen anime, continuing the Jade Shadows story arc. Wrestle for control of the 65th Warframe, Sirius & Orion , and take on their mentor Protoframes, Ryoku (Ash) and Vena (Garuda), while diving into new Railjack spaceship missions as well as other additions like the 50th Prime Warframe, Styanax Prime, other new cosmetics, weapons, and more.

Witness temporal conflicts boil in the Jade Shadows: Constellations gameplay trailer here

“ Warframe has always been elevated by our biggest and boldest media inspirations while remaining grounded in everyday human experiences we can all relate to,” said Rebecca Ford, Warframe Creative Director. “ Constellations felt like a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to up our own ante, surprise players with the return to a beloved character story, and a playable Warframe unlike anything we've created before. It’s both a love letter to hard-working single dads and the dramatic rivalries found in our favorite anime. We’re only halfway through the year, and still have so much more left in store for the Warframe community, so tune into TennoCon in July for the latest surprises!”

Additional details on new content arriving in Jade Shadows: Constellations :

Quell the quarrel between beings from competing timelines in the new Jade Shadows: Constellations Quest. Continue the story of Stalker and his child as two rival futures collide into the present. Witness the escalating conflict between Sirius and Orion, different versions of the same powerful entity fighting to preserve their existence at any cost. For the full list of Quest prerequisites, please see the Warframe Quest guide here .

Following Quest completion, forge an alliance with one of two Protoframe mentors , the keen Ryoku ( Ash ) or the cruel Vena ( Garuda ), and take flight in the latest Railjack spaceship missions at Uranus Proxima. Commandeer the chosen ally’s custom vessel, recruit them as a permanent Railjack crew member, and board the opposing Protoframe ship in an epic space battle

Untangle the 65th Warframe’s eternal rivalry between Sirius and Orion, two Warframes controlled as one. Seamlessly swap between each version of the 65th Warframe during combat. As Sirius, hurl his signature scythe across the battlefield to reap and sow while expanding a healing sanctuary to protect allies. As Orion, carve through foes with a devastating slash and summon a roaming black hole that draws enemies into its crushing center. Escalate their conflict through a shared ultimate ability, sending both rivals hurtling into a destructive aerial clash, annihilating anything caught in their path.

Styanax Prime Access enters the arena alongside this update. Forged in glory, Styanax , the indomitable peltast, joins the refined Prime ranks with increased energy and shields, as well as an extra Naramon polarity slot. Take up a new arsenal worthy of a seasoned warrior: the Afentis Prime Speargun and Athodai Prime Pistol, and adorn this champion with the latest Prime Syandana backpiece and Prime Armor attachments. Unlock any of these new Primes instantly in the Warframe Marketplace or earn them for free via gameplay. For more information about Prime Access, please visit the Prime Access FAQ .

Write a new page in the world of Warframe with new cosmetics, weapons, and ability retouches. Unleash a mythic new look to the scholar turned warrior, Dante , and his new Tytonis skin. Wield the infestation of Nidus , the adaptive scourge, once more to experiment with a fresh ability retouch, giving him more survivability and viability in combat. Step beyond familiar realities with the newest scythe melee weapon from either side, Pride, or Wrath, depending on what timeline it came from, Ryoku's and Vena's Protoframe hairstyles for the Operator/Drifter, four fresh Atragraph Mod designs to discover, and five new Incarnon weapons to master.

Prepare for TennoCon’s biggest reveals of the year on July 10-11 on Warframe’s official Twitch and YouTube channels. More details around Digital Extremes’ community celebration livestream content schedule for all things Warframe, please see the official website here .

For more information and full detailed patch notes on everyth ing arriving in Jade Shadows: Constellation today, please visit our official website here and ke ep an eye on our Bluesky , Twitter/X , Instagram , YouTube , Twitch , and TikTok accounts.

Warframe ® has evolved the looter-shooter genre since its debut on PC in 2013, becoming a multi-generational game with more than 85 million registered players across multiple console and mobile platforms. In this free sci-fi epic, players join an ancient class of warriors, the Tenno, and fight their way through an ever-expanding universe of more than 18 planets and 60 Warframes, with four open worlds to discover. With unrivaled customization, an intriguing narrative, and one of the most welcoming communities in gaming, players can define their playstyle and become an unstoppable force with a squad. Warframe ’s success has led to the creation of TennoCon, where thousands of fans descend upon the heart of Digital Extremes’ creative operations in London, Ontario, annually to discover the future of the game. For more information about Warframe , please visit: www.warframe.com .

Digital Extremes ranks as one of the world's top video game development studios. Originating 30 years ago with the co-creation of Epic Games' multi-million unit selling Unreal ® franchise, including Unreal and Unreal Tournament , Digital Extremes went on to develop Dark Sector ® , BioShock ® for the PlayStation ® 3, the BioShock 2 multiplayer campaign, and The Darkness ® II . The studio develops its games using its proprietary Evolution Engine and reached critical and commercial success with the free-to-play action game Warframe ® , supporting a global community of 85+ million registered players across all major core gaming platforms. Digital Extremes is currently developing its new IP – Soulframe ® – with additional updates coming soon. For more information about Digital Extremes, visit www.digitalextremes.com .

Outrageous PR Danielle Woodyatt & Ami Fleet press@outrageouspr.com

items.title: ‘Warframe’ “Jade Shadows: Constellations” Update Out Now
items.summary: Today, developer Digital Extremes launched their next major update & story chapter for their free-to-play looter shooter title 'Warframe', dubbed
items.content:
Mobile News Nintendo PC PlayStation Xbox Genghis Husameddin

Today, developer Digital Extremes launched their next major update & story chapter for their free-to-play looter shooter title ‘Warframe’, dubbed “Jade Shadows: Constellations”. Available now across PC, consoles, and mobile platforms, this update brings the game’s 65th Warframe: a “two playable characters in one” unit, Sirius and Orion, alongside new Protoframes. The latest narrative trailer also pays homage to old doods ahead of Father’s Day, as players Step back into the role of a newfound father, the Stalker, and bring an end to the fight between two versions of his son in an emotional Quest, inspired by adrenaline-pumping shounen anime, continuing the Jade Shadows story arc. We love you, dads!

“ Warframe has always been elevated by our biggest and boldest media inspirations while remaining grounded in everyday human experiences we can all relate to,” said Rebecca Ford , Warframe Creative Director. “ Constellations felt like a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to up our own ante, surprise players with the return to a beloved character story, and a playable Warframe unlike anything we’ve created before. It’s both a love letter to hard-working single dads and the dramatic rivalries found in our favorite anime. We’re only halfway through the year, and still have so much more left in store for the Warframe community, so tune into TennoCon in July for the latest surprises!”

Quell the quarrel between beings from competing timelines in the new Jade Shadows: Constellations Quest. Continue the story of Stalker and his child as two rival futures collide into the present. Witness the escalating conflict between Sirius and Orion, different versions of the same powerful entity fighting to preserve their existence at any cost. For the full list of Quest prerequisites, please see the Warframe Quest guide here .

Complete the new story Quest to unlock the latest Railjack spaceship missions in Uranus Proxima and seek out the latest Warframe weaponry:

Prepare Styanax Prime Access, arriving alongside Jade Shadows: Constellations :

Write a new page in the world of Warframe with new cosmetics, weapons, and ability retouches. Unleash a mythic new look to the scholar turned warrior, Dante , and his new Tytonis skin. Wield the infestation of Nidus , the adaptive scourge, once more to experiment with a fresh ability retouch, giving him more survivability and viability in combat. Step beyond familiar realities with the newest scythe melee weapon from either side, Pride, or Wrath, depending on what timeline it came from, Ryoku’s and Vena’s Protoframe hairstyles for the Operator/Drifter, four fresh Atragraph Mod designs to discover, and five new Incarnon weapons to master.

Warframe is available across PC via Warframe.com /Steam (Windows & Linux via Proton), Xbox, PlayStation, Nintendo Switch consoles, iOS, and Android devices. Full cross-save and crossplay are supported across all platforms.

Enjoying DayOne ? Do us a favour, and make us a ‘ Preferred Source on Google ‘ – that way, you’ll only be delivered more gaming coverage from our team.

Head over and let us know your thoughts in the Day O ne Forums , the perfect place to chat about games if you’re a little more old-school and prefer things in a longform format.

Prefer the chaos of live and fast moving conversation? Come and make some noise in the lively DayOne Community Discord Server instead. If you’re rocking a DayOne Membership, both places give special access for our supporters, and you’ll unlock a ton of exclusive content, just for you.

items.title: Styanax Prime Access
items.summary: Glory calls for Styanax! The master of shield and spear has ascended into Prime Access, offering the steadfastness of an entire phalanx to those bold enough to wield him. Clad in Orokin Gold and outfitted with Exalted manifestations of his
items.content:
Glory calls for Styanax! The master of shield and spear has ascended into Prime Access, offering the steadfastness of an entire phalanx to those bold enough to wield him. Clad in Orokin Gold and outfitted with Exalted manifestations of his iconic Tharros and Axios, this unbreakable champion arrives alongside his signature Prime Weapons and exclusive Prime Accessories. Take advantage of instant access and acquire the Prime items you want by selecting from the Weapons, Prime, Complete and Accessories Packs, depending on your platform and preferences. You can also earn Styanax Prime and his signature Prime Weapons in-game by collecting and cracking Void Relics! https://store.steampowered.com/app/4760260/Warframe_Styanax_Prime_Access__Weapons_Pack/ https://store.steampowered.com/bundle/78326/Warframe_Styanax_Prime_Access__Prime/ https://store.steampowered.com/bundle/78308/Warframe_Styanax_Prime_Access__Complete/ https://store.steampowered.com/app/4760270/Warframe_Styanax_Prime_Access__Accessories_Pack/ Styanax Prime Access includes:

Styanax Prime

Shield of the innocent. Spear of justice. Styanax Prime holds the line in defiance of tyranny.

Afentis Prime

The gilded speargun of Styanax Prime shines like a beacon across the battlefield, invigorating allies and stunning enemies.

Athodai Prime

Unleash the song of the righteous warrior with this gilded pistol that goes into Overdrive on headshot kills, maximizing fire rate and ammo efficiency for a short time.

Exclusive Styanax Prime Glyphs

Styanax Prime Accessories:

  • Lanex Prime Syandana
  • Daurus Prime Armor
  • Tharros and Axios Prime Decoration
  • 90-Day Affinity Booster
  • 90-Day Resource Booster
 Note: You'll find the best value in the Styanax Prime Access Complete pack, which includes the Styanax Prime Warframe, his Prime Weapons and Accessories, as well as Platinum!

WHAT ARE PRIMES?

When you require the finest and most powerful armaments, only Primes will do. Exquisitely gilded, every Prime Warframe, Weapon and item exists in its purest form — just as the Orokin intended. If you're new to Warframe, you can learn more about the Prime Access Program by visiting our official FAQs page and watching our community video guide.

items.title: Warframe Jade Shadows Constellations Update Released as Patch 2.51/1.059
items.summary: Warframe's big Jade Shadows Constellations update is live on June 17 as version 2.51/1.059, bringing new story content and more.
items.content:
The latest content drop for Warframe is here, as Digital Extremes ushers in new story content in the form of Warframe’s Jade Shadows Constellations update!

This is a hefty download across all platforms, and this brings a new warframe and a lot more.

Console gamers will see this download as Warframe update 2.51/1.059 , and we have the breakdown of new content below for an easier way to digest everything new.

The update introduces Warframe’s first “two-in-one” frame.

Each gains unique transformations and special effects.

For a deeper look into the new content, read on.

Following Quest completion, forge an alliance with one of two Protoframe mentors , the keen Ryoku (Ash) or the cruel Vena (Garuda), and take flight in the latest Railjack spaceship missions at Uranus Proxima. Commandeer the chosen ally’s custom vessel, recruit them as a permanent Railjack crew member, and board the opposing Protoframe ship in an epic space battle

Untangle the 65th Warframe’s eternal rivalry between Sirius and Orion, two Warframes controlled as one . Seamlessly swap between each version of the 65th Warframe during combat. As Sirius, hurl his signature scythe across the battlefield to reap and sow while expanding a healing sanctuary to protect allies. As Orion, carve through foes with a devastating slash and summon a roaming black hole that draws enemies into its crushing center. Escalate their conflict through a shared ultimate ability, sending both rivals hurtling into a destructive aerial clash, annihilating anything caught in their path.

Styanax Prime Access enters the arena alongside this update . Forged in glory, Styanax, the indomitable peltast, joins the refined Prime ranks with increased energy and shields, as well as an extra Naramon polarity slot. Take up a new arsenal worthy of a seasoned warrior: the Afentis Prime Speargun and Athodai Prime Pistol, and adorn this champion with the latest Prime Syandana backpiece and Prime Armor attachments. Unlock any of these new Primes instantly in the Warframe Marketplace or earn them for free via gameplay.

Write a new page in the world of Warframe with new cosmetics, weapons, and ability retouches . Unleash a mythic new look to the scholar turned warrior, Dante, and his new Tytonis skin. Wield the infestation of Nidus, the adaptive scourge, once more to experiment with a fresh ability retouch, giving him more survivability and viability in combat. Step beyond familiar realities with the newest scythe melee weapon from either side, Pride, or Wrath, depending on what timeline it came from, Ryoku’s and Vena’s Protoframe hairstyles for the Operator/Drifter, four fresh Atragraph Mod designs to discover, and five new Incarnon weapons to master.

The complete patch notes list all the changes, improvements, and more, which you can read about on the Warframe forums .

In related news, Warframe’s director, Rebecca Ford, has commented on Destiny 2’s live service support ending and says it doesn’t make sense.

RELATED Report: Unannounced The Witcher Multiplayer Game Slated for PC & Mobile; Gameplay Details Revealed We have a plethora of upcoming game projects for The Witcher franchise, such as the recently revealed DLC for The Witcher 3, which serves as a prelude to the highly anticipated The Witcher 4, and a Witcher 1 remake.

Stay connected to MP1st and the latest news by following us on Bluesky , X , Facebook , TikTok , YouTube , and Google News .

items.title: Jade Shadows: Constellations Available Now
items.summary: ***Warning: Significant spoilers for the original Jade Shadows Quest follow!*** A rivalry for the ages has officially landed with the arrival of Warframe's latest update, Jade Shadows: Constellations! NEW QUEST The consequences continue in
items.content:
***Warning: Significant spoilers for the original Jade Shadows Quest follow!*** A rivalry for the ages has officially landed with the arrival of Warframe's latest update, Jade Shadows: Constellations!

NEW QUEST

The consequences continue in a new cinematic sequel Quest that picks up where 2024's Jade Shadows left off. Reassume control of the Stalker as he allies once more with the Tenno to navigate a conflict of celestial proportions. His dueling sons from parallel futures have come to the Origin System in search of their father, and will stop at nothing to claim him for their respective timelines. Only you can end their cycle of conflict and bring harmony to the Origin System!

NEW WARFRAME: SIRIUS & ORION

Dedicated warriors borne of relentless determination. Sirius & Orion comprise the very first dual Warframe, occupying the same slot in your Arsenal while jockeying for position on the battlefield as they rain cosmic destruction upon foes. With their signature Pride and Wrath Scythes you can use brutal heavy attacks to gain Overguard and increased Critical Chance.

Sirius Ability 1: Coronal Ejection

Hurl Sirius' Jade Light infused scythe, dealing Heat Damage while collecting any pickups in its path. Hold to swap to Orion and use Gravitic Slash.

Orion Ability 1: Gravitic Slash

Repel enemies with Orion’s sinister scythe, dealing Slash Damage while reducing shields and armor. Hold to swap to Sirius and use Coronal Ejection.

Sirius Ability 2: Jade Stars

Sirius conjures Jade Light motes that slowly regenerate over time. Attacking enemies launches the motes, dealing Heat Damage. Hold to swap to Orion and use Astral Shell.

Orion Ability 2: Astral Shell

Envelop Orion in an Astral Shell. Upon taking damage the shell becomes a decoy that draws fire until it's destroyed. Hold to swap to Sirius and use Jade Stars.

Sirius Ability 3: Light’s Sanctuary

Sirius creates a well of light that heals and revives allies, while reducing incoming damage. The well slowly grows in size and power. Hold to swap to Orion and use Event Horizon.

Orion Ability 3: Event Horizon

Orion forms a drifting black hole, trapping enemies within its gravity. Hitting the black hole with Gravitic Slash or Coronal Ejection will extend its duration and change its trajectory: Gravitic Slash will direct it away from Orion, and Coronal Ejection will pull it towards Sirius. Hold to swap to Sirius and use Light's Sanctuary.

Sirius & Orion Ability 4: Celestial Clash

Sirius & Orion take to the skies in a cosmic duel. Each attack consumes a Constellation Star to inflict colossal collateral Blast damage. Match the star's color to the Warframes color to gain increased Critical Chance.

NEW PROTOFRAMES: RYOKU AND VENA

Sirius & Orion haven't arrived on their own. Both have trained extensively under the guidance and mentorship of two equally ruthless fighters mired in an intense rivalry of their own. The Ash Protoframe, Ryoku, is a discerning assassin who delivers violence with a disturbing discipline that he has passed along to Sirius. His counterpart, the Garuda Protoframe Vena, indulges her bloodlust with a frenzied brutality befitting her "Wytch" moniker, which she has likewise instilled in Orion.

RETURN TO RAILJACK

Completing the Quest will unlock the Uranus Proxima which includes our most accessible Railjack Missions to date. Here there's no requirement to level up — or even own — a Railjack of your own! Simply enter the Pontis Tower hub, decide whether you'd prefer to fight for Ryoku or Vena, and board their stylized vessels to engage in some of Warframe's most epic space combat yet.

DANTE TYTONIS COLLECTION

Knowledge glides on dark wings with the Dante Tytonis Skin, Noctua Lophos and Gallistrix Warfan Skin. An appropriately avian-themed Collection for the venerable scribe Warframe.

NEW INCARNONS

Five new weapons have been altered by the infernal power of the Void for your destructive pleasure. Wield new Incarnon versions of the Ballistica (Crossbow), Destreza (Rapier), Obex (Sparring), Stug (Pistol), and Vectis (Sniper Rifle), each more indomitable than ever before.

QUALITY OF LIFE IMPROVEMENTS

Nidus Retouch

The avatar of the Infested, Nidus, is more pestilent than ever thanks to an Ability Retouch. Bestowed with significant health boosts and other improvements, this walking plague is both more accessible and enjoyable to wield on the battlefield.

Additional Improvements

As ever, we're improving the overall Warframe experience with several new additions and upgrades, including overhauled lighting in Corpus Ships, adding Damage Over Time (DOT) to enemy Health Bars, enhanced Junction Bosses, Camera offset options, Companion XP adjustments, four new Arcanes and more.

JADE SHADOWS: CONSTELLATIONS PACKS

https://store.steampowered.com/app/4735390/Warframe_Constellations_Complete_Collection/ Reinforce your Arsenal with the newest Warframe, Weapons and Customizations now available alongside Jade Shadows: Constellations!

items.title: Equinox Prime and Wukong Prime Enter Prime Resurgence
items.summary: Equinox Prime From the far edge of fate between day and night, she rises. Equinox Prime harnesses the power of duality to support others — offensively in her Day form and defensively with her Night form. For the heart that seeks equilibrium
items.content: Equinox Prime From the far edge of fate between day and night, she rises. Equinox Prime harnesses the power of duality to support others — offensively in her Day form and defensively with her Night form. For the heart that seeks equilibrium, there’s arguably no better fit than Equinox Prime. Wukong Prime Trickery’s simian avatar possesses strength equal only to his mischievous nature. Survivability is what Wukong Prime is all about, with several of his Abilities allowing him to cheat death or create celestial twins that draw attention from enemies. If keeping foes on their toes is your thing, Wukong Prime should do the trick.

items.title: As Destiny 2 waves goodbye in its final update, Warframe commemorates the beloved Bungie MMO with an in-game reward
items.summary: "Love seeing communities sharing stories and love for their games," says Bungie
items.content:
"Love seeing communities sharing stories and love for their games," says Bungie's communications lead

When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. Here’s how it works .

(Image credit: Bungie) Copy link Facebook X Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Warframe is commemorating Destiny 2 with an upcoming in-game reward made in honor of the MMO as Bungie releases its final big update.

Destiny 2 waved goodbye with its final update that dropped earlier this week. Although it'll remain playable, Monument of Triumph marks the long-running game's supposedly last piece of new content, and with no current plans for Destiny 3 at the studio , the update could potentially be the entire series' last hurrah for years to come.

But Warframe's not letting Destiny 2 sail into the Undying Lands without throwing it some figurative flowers. In response to one fan asking for a commemorative player title, Warframe director Rebb Ford tweeted that the team would "figure something out as fast as we can" a few days ago.

Alas, a few days later, Ford has made good on her promise: "On June 17, players can play an Alert on Telesto to Make Their Own Fate in Warframe. More details to follow," she writes alongside a screenshot of a new Honoria, Make Your Own Fate.

On June 17, players can play an Alert on Telesto to Make Their Own Fate in Warframe <3. More details to follow! https://t.co/ADMJc5MbAN pic.twitter.com/mE3SSG5uCj June 10, 2026

Fans also celebrate the fact that the new alert would take place on Telesto, which is also a pivotal location in Destiny lore and is the Saturn moon the game's iconic fusion rifle is named after, making this an extra sweet tribute to Bungie's MMO FPS.

Warframe leads join fans mourning Destiny 2: "There is no Warframe without the legacy of Bungie" Destiny 2 lead says the final update won't just fade out: "Yes, we get to say goodbye" Destiny 2 community lead thanks "everyone who has reached out" as Bungie ends the game after 9 years "Shout out to Warframe folks sending kind words and love to Guardians," Destiny's principal communications manager, Dylan 'dmg04' Gafner, also writes . "Love seeing communities sharing stories and love for their games."

This isn't the first time Warframe devs have honored Destiny 2 – the looter shooter developers themselves previously noted how Bungie's shooter had an outsized impact on their work . "There is no Warframe without the legacy of Bungie games," Ford wrote in response to the game's final update. "Destiny was and is a force of nature, loved and held in the hands of so many people who, for a moment, were part of the biggest thing in gaming."

Weekly digests, tales from the communities you love, and more

While Destiny 2's sudden end is sad, at least the game is going out on top. Players turned up to say goodbye in numbers not seen since Destiny 2's The Final Shape expansion , and the shooter even managed to top Steam's top-sellers chart for a while.

For now, Bungie's working on seasonal updates for Marathon and is reportedly pitching new projects. Layoffs are also planned, it's claimed .

Destiny 2 finally has the all-in-one collection Bungie should've released years ago, and it's just $25 right now.

items.title: Jade Shadows: Constellations Packs Coming Soon
items.summary: Reinforce your Arsenal with the newest Warframe, Weapons and Customizations launching alongside Jade Shadows: Constellations! Navigate the conflict between clashing rivals and their mentors as you take up the mantle of the Stalker once more
items.content:
Reinforce your Arsenal with the newest Warframe, Weapons and Customizations launching alongside Jade Shadows: Constellations! Navigate the conflict between clashing rivals and their mentors as you take up the mantle of the Stalker once more. Jade Shadows: Constellations launches June 17 on all platforms. Now you can take these dueling reflections home by purchasing one of our latest Supporter Packs — either with your local currency from Warframe.com or the in-game Market for Platinum!

Constellations Sirius & Orion Bundle

What might have been becomes reality with Sirius & Orion. Assume the full might of dueling dark futures and follow the stars to constellations of combined power. Featuring:
  • Sirius & Orion Warframe*
  • Pride (Scythe Weapon)
  • Wrath (Scythe Weapon)
  • Constellations Fusion Display
  • 190 Platinum (if purchased with real-world currency)
*Please note that Sirius & Orion are a single Warframe and not two separate Warframes.

Constellations Gemini Collection

Fight an eternal battle for the future with Vena, the blood-loving fanatic, and Ryoku, the cool and calculating killer. Two bitter rivals brought together in this collection of Protoframe Gemini Skins. Each skin can switch between Warframe and Protoframe appearances by activating the corresponding Gemini Emote. Featuring:
  • Ryoku Gemini Skin
  • Ryoku Gemini Emote
  • Vena Gemini Skin
  • Vena Gemini Emote
  • 375 Platinum (if purchased with real-world currency)

Dante Tytonis Collection

Choose the path of wisdom with the Dante Tytonis Collection. His dark wings harbor secret knowledge and arcane power. Featuring:
  • Dante Tytonis Skin
  • Noctua Lophos (Dante's Exalted Tome)
  • Gallistrix Warfan Skin

Constellations Complete Collection

Confront the legacy of a doomed future. Is there room for harmony in the present? Featuring:
  • Constellations Sirius & Orion Bundle
  • Constellations Gemini Collection

Razor's Edge Salon Pack

The future might be doomed, but style doesn’t have to be with this collection featuring Ryoku and Vena’s hairstyles for the Operator and Drifter. Find these bundles in the in-game Market with full regional pricing when Jade Shadows: Constellations launches on June 17!

items.title: Devstream 196 Overview
items.summary: Our latest Devstream revealed the June 17 release date for Jade Shadows: Constellations! We dove deep into the details surrounding our first-ever dual Warframe, Sirius & Orion, as well as the unique new combat experience offered by their st
items.content:
Our latest Devstream revealed the June 17 release date for Jade Shadows: Constellations!

We dove deep into the details surrounding our first-ever dual Warframe, Sirius & Orion, as well as the unique new combat experience offered by their star-powered Abilities and signature Pride and Wrath weapons. We also took to the stars with Ryoku and Vena for a closer look at the latest Railjack Missions in the new Uranus Proxima. Somehow along the way we found time to discuss Styanax Prime, Mesa Heirloom, Summer Game Fest and, of course, TennoCon 2026. Check out our full Overview for all the details you may have missed.

items.title: Jade Shadows: Constellations Coming June 17
items.summary: Warframe's highly-praised 2024 cinematic arc continues in an unexpected follow-up once again centered on the Stalker as he navigates a conflict raging between two bitter rivals from doomed futures, Sirius and Orion. NEW WARFRAME: SIRIUS & O
items.content:
Warframe's highly-praised 2024 cinematic arc continues in an unexpected follow-up once again centered on the Stalker as he navigates a conflict raging between two bitter rivals from doomed futures, Sirius and Orion.

NEW WARFRAME: SIRIUS & ORION

Sirius has honed his skills under the tutelage of a cunning assassin named Ryoku, the Ash Protoframe. He fights not only for control of the Stalker, but also the destruction of his parallel self, Orion. Orion, meanwhile, has sharpened his skills under the vengeful eye of a bloodthirsty mercenary named Vena, the Garuda Protoframe. He seeks the Stalker and the end of Sirius, whom he perceives as far too tolerant of evil. Together they comprise the Origin System's first unified, dual Warframe with a veritable constellation of cosmic Abilities designed to rain destruction upon foes. Combined with their Pride and Wrath signature Heavy Scythes, they offer a unique combat experience harnessed straight from the power of the stars.

RETURN TO RAILJACK

Completing the Jade Shadows: Constellations Quest will unlock a new region of the outer Origin System: the Uranus Proxima. Featuring our most accessible Railjack Missions to date, there's no requirement to level up — or even own — a Railjack of your own to access them! Simply enter the Pontis Tower hub, decide whether you'd prefer to fight for Ryoku or Vena, and board their heavily modified ships to engage in some of Warframe's most epic space combat yet.

BELLY OF THE BEAST ENDING JUNE 1

While you won't uncover new tales of the Stalker until June 17, you can still aid him in his efforts to defend the Jade Light from the clutches of Parvos Granum in Operation: Belly of the Beast. Join with your clanmates to earn new and returning Rewards until June 1 at 2 p.m. ET.
